title: werewolf-minigame-stub e2e test fixture — Shadow Mirroring verification
description: Create e2e test fixture verifying the complete Shadow Mirroring pipeline on examples/werewolf-minigame-stub. Tests cover: (1) forensic.ts snapshot tests for assertions[] and candidate_files[] output format; (2) init-context schema validation for confidence_snapshot and topology_type fields; (3) sync-meta scanning only .fabric/agents/**/*.md with correct layer/topology_type derivation; (4) business directories ZERO AGENTS.md verification. These tests ensure the refactored system produces the expected Shadow Mirroring output and prevents regressions.

files.target: Existing test file or new test file for forensic assertions
files.change: Add snapshot tests for buildAssertions() on werewolf-minigame-stub: assert assertions[] contains Cocos framework assertion (confidence=HIGH, coverage.ratio≥0.8), @ccclass pattern assertion (confidence=HIGH), .meta file protection recommendation (confidence=HIGH). Add snapshot tests for buildCandidateFiles(): assert candidate_files contains entry/component/config family representatives; total count ≤ 12.

Schema parse tests for ForensicAssertion and CandidateFileEntry: forward compat (with all new fields), backward compat (legacy format with only recommendations_for_skill). Include a complete mock forensicReport with assertions[] and candidate_files[] round-tripping through schema.

Add tests: (1) sync-meta on werewolf fixture after TASK-006 .fabric/agents/ structure created — verify only .fabric/agents/**/*.md files scanned, zero colocated AGENTS.md included. (2) deriveLayer() unit tests for all path patterns. (3) deriveTopologyType() unit tests. (4) backward-compat migration: old agents.meta.json without layer/topology_type fields upgraded without data loss.

Schema validation tests: parse init-context.json with confidence_snapshot and topology_type fields (forward compat); parse without new fields (backward compat). Assert InitContextInvariant accepts HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW confidence_snapshot. Assert InitContextDomainGroup accepts mirror/cross-cutting topology_type.

implementation: Read existing test files in packages/cli/src/scanner/ and packages/cli/src/commands/ to understand test framework and conventions
Read examples/werewolf-minigame-stub structure to understand fixture files available for testing
Write forensic.test.ts assertion tests: import buildAssertions/buildCandidateFiles, run on werewolf fixture path, assert minimum field presence and confidence levels
Write forensic-report.test.ts schema parse tests for forward and backward compatibility
Write sync-meta.test.ts tests for new scan scope (only .fabric/agents/) and deriveLayer/deriveTopologyType unit tests
Write init-context-schema.test.ts for new optional fields
Run test suite to confirm all tests pass: pnpm test --filter cli; pnpm test --filter shared

reference.examples: conclusions.json recommendations[4].steps provides specific test assertions: 3 HIGH assertions for cocos framework, candidate_files with entry/component/config families, sync-meta correctness with .fabric/agents/ only scan

criteria: forensic.test.ts: buildAssertions() on werewolf fixture emits ≥ 3 HIGH confidence assertions (cocos-creator framework, @ccclass pattern, .meta protection)
forensic.test.ts: buildCandidateFiles() on werewolf fixture returns ≤ 12 candidates covering ≥ 3 families
forensic-report.test.ts: schema.parse() with new fields succeeds; schema.parse() without new fields succeeds
sync-meta.test.ts: deriveLayer() returns L0/L1/L2 for respective path patterns (4 cases pass)
sync-meta.test.ts: deriveTopologyType() returns mirror for normal path, cross-cutting for _cross/ path
sync-meta.test.ts: after TASK-006 fixture setup, sync-meta scans produce zero colocated AGENTS.md entries
init-context-schema.test.ts: parse with and without confidence_snapshot/topology_type both succeed
pnpm test for packages/cli and packages/shared passes with zero failures

rationale.chosen_approach: Co-located test files following existing test placement conventions; use werewolf fixture as integration test target since it is the primary reference project
alternatives_considered: Separate integration test directory (more setup overhead; existing convention uses co-located tests)
decision_factors: Tests must run in CI without external dependencies
werewolf fixture is already present and maintained as example project
TASK-006 creates .fabric/agents/ structure that these tests verify
rationale.tradeoffs: Tests depend on TASK-001/TASK-003/TASK-006 being complete; this task should be executed last among the schema/scanner/fixture tasks

success_metrics: 100% of new test cases pass on first run
Zero regressions in existing test suite
integration: forensic.test.ts: buildForensicReport integration test on werewolf fixture
sync-meta.test.ts: computeAgentsMeta integration test on werewolf fixture after TASK-006

risks.description: Test file locations or naming conventions may differ from expectations
risks.mitigation: Read existing test files before writing new ones to follow established conventions
risks.fallback: Place tests in dedicated tests/ subdirectory if co-located not allowed by project config

risks.description: werewolf fixture must have .fabric/agents/ structure (TASK-006) before sync-meta integration tests can run
risks.mitigation: TASK-007 is scheduled to depend on TASK-001, TASK-003, and TASK-006; do not execute until all dependencies complete
risks.fallback: Create minimal stub .fabric/agents/ structure inline within the test if TASK-006 is not yet complete
